The elementary claim for any given level of the market in T periods
may be constructed in a similar manner. Given the call prices, c (X, T),
prices of elementary claims on aggregate wealth, P(M, T), must be
those computed from the replicating portfolio of calls. The portfolio
giving $1.00 only if the market is M at T consists of one long call with X
= M - 1, one long with X = M + 1, and two short calls with X = M.
